summary: Patient position significantly impacts venous air embolism risk by modifying intrathoracic pressure and airflow direction; Trendelenberg positions reduce this risk by directing air away from systemic circulation into less dangerous areas like the right heart chamber. This highlights a crucial role for surgical positioning in minimizing complications related to intraoperative air entry.
